page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose cloud server powered by an AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz on an arm64 architecture. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across most workloads. The server achieves average results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations. Notably, it demonstrates strong memory performance, scoring in the top 25% on the Passmark memory test, with a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 775.64 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in specific areas, scoring in the bottom 25% for Passmark CPU encryption and bzip3 decompression.

With its dedicated ARM-based cores, integrated NVMe storage, and strong memory bandwidth, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage without requiring expensive GPU accelerators. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching. It is less optimal for heavy cryptographic tasks or complex medium-to-large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server powered by an 8-core AWS Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers average CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks, alongside strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% of the Passmark memory test. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. It is suited for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads, though it shows weaker performance in cryptographic and bzip3 decompression tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.2xlarge is an 8-core ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 32 GB RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, and strong memory performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.2xlarge is an 8-core ARM server with 32 GB RAM and 474 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong memory performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Neoverse-V1 ARM processor running at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 32 GB of RAM and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% on Passmark memory tests
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
